overview: Promote the prior feedback-scoped 'serverAggregates' design into a first-class variable primitive: a SERVER-COMPUTED VARIABLE is an ordinary entry in `questionnaire.variables: Variable[]` (packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts:56-73) that carries a new optional `server?: ServerComputationDef` block instead of (or alongside) a `formula`. Because it lives in the same array, `QuestionnaireRuntime.initializeVariables()` (apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:583) registers it into the one live VariableEngine with zero consumer changes — flow conditions (findMatchingFlowRule, :1541), piping ({{var}}/${var} via apps/web/src/lib/services/variableInterpolation.ts), quotas (QuotaService.evaluateQuotaCondition), scripting, and the statistical-feedback engine's dotted-path walker (resolveVariableValue, engine.ts:104) all resolve it exactly like any other variable. Its VALUE is computed server-side by a new SECURITY DEFINER function `fillout_dataset_stats` (00028's `fillout_cohort_stats` generalized with designer-declared dataset filters + full quartiles), fetched by an anonymous-safe batch endpoint keyed to the PUBLISHED definition (the client never sends filters), cached version-pinned in a new Dexie table, and injected into the VariableEngine via `setVariable(id, value, 'server-sync')` at runtime construction — so it resolves OFFLINE from the last synced value, falling back to the variable's own `defaultValue` (existing registerVariable semantics, VariableEngine.ts:46-53) when never synced. Two materializations: `type:'number'` + `server.stat:'mean'` → scalar (`cohortMeanAnxiety > 40` works in flow/quota/formula); `type:'object'` (no `stat`) → full stats bundle `{n,mean,sd,min,max,p10,p25,median,p75,p90,p95,p99,computedAt}` for feedback charts, mirroring the `score.<scaleId>` object precedent (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1565-1613). The E-FEEDBACK-3 report page (`settings.report: ReportPageConfig`, same definition-JSONB precedent as `settings.scoring` at questionnaire.ts:293-296) is a pure CONSUMER: its box-cohort widget binds `comparison.serverVariable: 'cohortAnxiety'` and reads the object out of the completed session's variables — no network on the render path. Auto-recalculation = server recomputes on every fetch past a ~300s Redis TTL; clients refresh at page load, on pin-resume, and after each successful FilloutUploadSync drain (so the cohort that just absorbed this participant updates promptly). Anonymity: only designer-published filters are evaluable and the existing MIN_COHORT_N=5 floor (apps/server/src/api/sessions/models.rs:1361) withholds stats per declaration.
data_model: packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts — `Variable` gains `server?: ServerComputationDef`. ServerComputationDef { source: 'variable'|'response'; key: string /* e.g. 'score.anxiety.value' or a question id */; stat?: ServerStat /* present ⇒ scalar materialization; requires variable.type==='number'. Absent ⇒ full stats object; requires type==='object' */; dataset?: DatasetFilter; staleAfterMs?: number /* default 30d, render-anyway-with-warning threshold */ }. ServerStat = 'n'|'mean'|'sd'|'min'|'max'|'p10'|'p25'|'median'|'p75'|'p90'|'p95'|'p99'. DatasetFilter { id: string; label?: string; versionScope?: 'any'|'sameMajor'|'exact' (default 'sameMajor'); completedAfter?: string; completedBefore?: string; where?: Array<{ var: string; op: 'eq'|'ne'|'lt'|'lte'|'gt'|'gte'|'in'; value: string|number|Array<string|number> }> } — where-clauses restricted to session variables in v1 (indexed via 00012 session_variable_index). The variable's existing `defaultValue` IS the offline/no-data fallback — registerVariable already seeds it (packages/scripting-engine/src/VariableEngine.ts:46-53). Constraint enforced at validation: server-variable `name` must be a plain identifier (mathjs symbol), max 50 server variables per questionnaire.

packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts — QuestionnaireSettings gains `report?: ReportPageConfig` (E-FEEDBACK-3; precedent settings.scoring at :293-296, settings.quotas at :291). ReportPageConfig { enabled; title?; layout: { columns: 12; rowHeight; gap }; widgets: ReportWidget[]; enablePdfDownload?; refreshMs? /* default 24h fetch-skip window */ }. ReportWidget { id; type: 'score-tile'|'bar'|'box-cohort'|'radar-profile'|'distribution-with-marker'|'gauge'|'interpretive-text'|'results-table'|'completion-meta'; position (shape copied from apps/web/src/lib/analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts — copied, not imported; analytics widgets are authenticated online tools); binding: { source: 'variable'|'score'; key; field? }; comparison?: { source: 'none'|'norm-table'|'custom-norm'|'self-baseline'|'server-variable'; serverVariable?: string /* NAME of an object-typed server variable */; normTableId?; fallback: 'hide'|'norm-table'|'message'; fallbackNormTableId? }; interpretation?; text? }.

packages/questionnaire-core (new file src/serverVariables.ts, exported from index) — pure helpers shared by designer, server-response mapping, and sync: collectServerVariables(q: Questionnaire): Variable[] (filter v.server, dedupe, cap check), declHash(def: ServerComputationDef): string (canonical JSON, stable key order — enables safe cross-version cache fallback when a declaration is byte-identical), materializeServerValue(variable, row): unknown (scalar via def.stat vs full stats object; returns undefined when the needed stat is null so callers skip setVariable and defaultValue rules).

apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts — Dexie version(5) (version(4) is current at :253) adds `filloutServerVariables: '[definitionKey+variableId], definitionKey, questionnaireId, syncedAt'`; all v4 stores restated unchanged, no .upgrade() body. Row: { definitionKey /* filloutDefinitionKey(qid,maj,min,patch), :91 — same composite as filloutQuestionnaires */, variableId, name, declHash, questionnaireId, n, stats: { mean, stdDev, min, max, p10, p25, median, p75, p90, p95, p99 } | null, computedAt /* server clock */, syncedAt /* client clock */ }.

apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/engine.ts — StatisticalSourceMode (:20) gains 'server-variable'; StatisticalFeedbackDataSourceConfig gains `serverVariable?: string` and `fallbackNormTableId?: string`; validateStatisticalFeedbackConfig gains the branch (requires currentVariable + serverVariable naming an object-typed server variable).

apps/server/src/api/sessions/models.rs — ServerVariablesResponse { questionnaire_id, version, computed_at, variables: Vec<ServerVariableEntry> }; ServerVariableEntry { id, name, source, key, dataset_id: Option<String>, decl_hash, sample_count: usize, stats: Option<NumericStatsSummary> } — NumericStatsSummary already carries p10/p25/p50/p75 slots (analytics.rs:630-662 currently nulls them); utoipa-annotated for contracts regen.
server_changes: apps/server/migrations/00030_dataset_stats.sql (verified head is 00029_sessions_insert_user_id_guard.sql) — CREATE FUNCTION public.fillout_dataset_stats(qid uuid, p_source text, p_key text, p_filter jsonb) RETURNS TABLE(n bigint, mean float8, std_dev float8, min float8, max float8, p10 float8, p25 float8, median float8, p75 float8, p90 float8, p95 float8, p99 float8). Body = 00028_cohort_stats.sql vals CTE + JSONB numeric coercion verbatim (both response and variable branches, apps/server/migrations/00028_cohort_stats.sql:59-144) plus: p10/p25/p75 percentile_cont; version-scope predicates on s.questionnaire_version_major/minor/patch driven by p_filter->>'versionScope' + the caller-resolved version components; completedAfter/Before bounds on s.completed_at; and per where-clause an EXISTS subquery over session_variable_index (svi2.variable_name = w->>'var', op applied to numeric_value or raw_value text) iterated via jsonb_array_elements(p_filter->'where'). Identical posture to 00028: LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_temp; OWNER TO qdesigner; REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC; GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app. fillout_cohort_stats stays untouched (live 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' modes keep working).

apps/server/src/api/sessions/analytics.rs — new handler public_server_variables, structured exactly like public_cohort_stats (:584): (1) published gate — status='published' AND deleted_at IS NULL else 404, no existence probing (:601-611 pattern); (2) resolve the definition JSONB — questionnaire_definitions.content for latest, questionnaire_snapshots (00010) when ?version=maj.min.patch targets a pinned version (snapshot gone → evaluate the latest registry and set a `fallback_registry: true` response flag); (3) extract server-variable declarations from content->'variables' (the same JSONB the by-code query already selects at apps/server/src/api/questionnaires.rs:133) — entries whose objects carry a `server` key, cap 50; THIS is the authorization model: only designer-published filters are ever evaluated, the anonymous client sends zero filter data; (4) run fillout_dataset_stats per declaration on &state.pool (no RLS GUC needed — owner-definer bypasses, as documented at analytics.rs:613-616); (5) MIN_COHORT_N=5 floor per entry (models.rs:1361): n<5 → sample_count populated, stats None; (6) respond { questionnaire_id, version, computed_at, variables } including decl_hash per entry.
apps/server/src/api/mod.rs — .route("/{id}/server-variables", get(sessions::public_server_variables)) beside the cohort-stats route at :180, same public/anonymous router (inherits rate limiting).

Redis TTL result cache via state.redis — key srvvars:{qid}:{maj.min.patch}, TTL ~300s, in-memory fallback mirroring the rate-limiter pattern; bounds anonymous load spikes to one ≤50-declaration fan-out per 5 minutes per version. This TTL is also the server-side 'auto-recalculate' cadence.
apps/server/src/openapi.rs — register the new path + ServerVariablesResponse/ServerVariableEntry schemas; regenerate @qdesigner/contracts so the web client types the fetch.
variable_engine_changes: packages/scripting-engine/src/VariableEngine.ts — NO structural change required (deliberate): server variables enter through the existing registerVariable()/setVariable(id, value, 'server-sync') API; createEvaluationScope (:283-300) already exposes both id and name to mathjs formulas, and importState/exportState (:167-174) already round-trips injected values through the E-FLOW-3 resume path. One additive tweak: accept 'server' as a recognized `source` string in VariableValue docs and add a test proving an object-valued variable supports dot access (`cohortAnxiety.mean > 40`) under createSandboxedMath.

apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/QuestionnaireRuntime.ts — RuntimeConfig (:52) gains `serverVariables?: Record<string, ServerVariableSnapshot>` (variableId → { value: unknown; n: number; computedAt: string; stale: boolean }). initializeVariables() (:583) gains a final injection pass: for each questionnaire variable with `v.server`, look up config.serverVariables[v.id]; when present, materializeServerValue() and this.variableEngine.setVariable(v.id, value, 'server-sync') — skipped (defaultValue rules) when absent or the requested stat is null (below the n≥5 floor). Object-typed variables always get the bundle including n and computedAt so feedback widgets can caption honestly even below-floor. Also register a `_serverDataAsOf` convenience global (mirrors the `_participantId` pattern at :592-598) carrying the oldest computedAt for piping into disclaimers.

apps/web/src/lib/fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime.ts — FilloutRuntimeConfig (:12) gains the same `serverVariables?` field, passed through into the wrapped RuntimeConfig at :95. No other change: complete() already snapshots getAllVariables() into session.variables (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1638-1645) so synced server values persist offline-first with everything else.

Consumers get the variable for free — verified against real call sites: flow conditions evaluate via this.variableEngine.evaluateFormula(rule.condition) (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1541-1551); piping resolves via the variables record (apps/web/src/lib/services/variableInterpolation.ts:13-47, FormulaParser/ASTEvaluator handle both simple refs and expressions); the statistical-feedback dotted-path walker resolveVariableValue (engine.ts:104) walks into the object bundle ('cohortAnxiety.mean'); QuotaService.evaluateQuotaCondition string-matches scalar variables.
sync_changes: apps/web/src/lib/fillout/services/FilloutContentCache.ts — new static cacheServerVariables(questionnaireData, version?): GET /api/questionnaires/{id}/server-variables[?version=maj.min.patch], bulkPut rows into filloutServerVariables keyed [definitionKey+variableId] with declHash from the response (cross-checked against the local declaration via the questionnaire-core declHash helper); SKIP the network when the newest row for the definitionKey is younger than settings.report?.refreshMs ?? 24h. pruneDefinitions() (:141) gains one clause: delete filloutServerVariables rows whose definitionKey it just GC'd (protectedVersionKeys at :254-273 keeps pinned/unsynced sessions' rows).

apps/web/src/routes/(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.ts — online branch, immediately after `await FilloutContentCache.cacheQuestionnaire(payload, code)` (:64): `await Promise.race([FilloutContentCache.cacheServerVariables(payload), timeout(3000)]).catch(() => {})` — first visit lands values before the participant can reach any consumer; failure never blocks load. Pin branch (:181-203): when a resume resolves a pinned older version while online, fire cacheServerVariables(effective, pin) so the pinned definitionKey gets fresh values for ITS declarations.

apps/web/src/routes/(fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte — before FilloutRuntime construction (:584): one Dexie read `db.filloutServerVariables.where('definitionKey').equals(key).toArray()` builds Record<variableId, ServerVariableSnapshot>; a miss falls back to a row from another definitionKey of the same questionnaireId ONLY when declHash matches (byte-identical declaration → semantically safe); result threaded as `serverVariables` into the FilloutRuntime config alongside resumeSnapshot/resumeFrom. At the CompletionScreen mount (:889): pass `variables` (from completedSession.variables — server values are in there by construction), `reportConfig={rawDefinition.settings?.report}`.

apps/web/src/lib/fillout/services/FilloutUploadSync.ts — after a successful syncNow() drain (:73), fire-and-forget cacheServerVariables refresh for the active definitionKey: the cohort that just absorbed this participant's completed session updates promptly for the next run on this device. Uploads unchanged.
apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts — Dexie version(5) with filloutServerVariables (additive; same pattern as the v3→v4 bump at :253).

Offline correctness invariant: after the injection pass, ZERO fetches exist anywhere on the read path — flow, piping, quotas, feedback, and the report page all read the in-memory VariableEngine. A device that has never been online with values simply resolves each server variable to its defaultValue; staleness (row older than server.staleAfterMs) still renders (offline correctness beats freshness) with the stale flag surfaced for captions.
designer_ux: Three touchpoints, all in the existing designer surface. (1) apps/web/src/lib/components/designer/VariableManager.svelte — the add/edit form (currently name/type/scope/defaultValue/formula, :22-66) gains a computation-mode selector: 'Static / Formula / Server-computed'. Choosing Server-computed reveals: source (variable | response), key picker (question ids, declared variables, score.<scaleId>.<field> paths from settings.scoring), materialization ('single statistic' → stat dropdown, forces type number; 'full statistics' → forces type object), dataset filter builder (version scope, completed date range, where-rows over session variables with op+value), and a REQUIRED defaultValue prompt framed as 'offline / insufficient-data fallback'. Inline validation: identifier-safe name, ≤50 server variables, where-vars must exist, stat/type coherence. A note pane states 'values compute after publish; participants see the value as of their last online load' — no live preview in v1 (the public endpoint only evaluates published declarations). (2) apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/statistical-feedback/StatisticalFeedbackDesigner.svelte — new source mode 'Server variable (offline-synced)' listing object-typed server variables; picking one sets dataSource.serverVariable + currentVariable + optional fallbackNormTableId; deprecation hint steers designers from the online-only 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' modes toward it. (3) New ReportPageEditor panel (settings surface, sibling of QuotaPanel.svelte) editing settings.report: widget palette, 12-column grid positions, per-widget binding to variables/scores and comparison.serverVariable, fallback policy per widget. Everywhere the variable appears (formula autocomplete via MonacoConfig, flow-rule editor, piping helper), it is just a variable — the designer needs no new mental model beyond 'this one is computed on the server from everyone's data'.
consumer_wiring: All consumers read the ONE VariableEngine the runtime populates (getVariableEngine(), QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:579), so wiring is uniform and mostly zero-diff. FEEDBACK: the statistical-feedback engine's new 'server-variable' branch resolves the participant value via resolveVariableValue(currentVariable, variables) (engine.ts:104, unchanged) and the cohort bundle via resolveVariableValue(dataSource.serverVariable, variables) — the object {n,mean,sd,min,max,p25,median,p75,...,computedAt} maps into the existing ChartSeriesContract (buildParticipantVsCohortSeriesLocal pattern) → FeedbackChart 'box' draws quartile box, min/max whiskers, mean±sd band, participant marker, caption 'n=142, as of <computedAt>' — no fetch, unlike today's 'cohort' modes that call sessionAnalyticsService.aggregatePublic live (engine.ts:481-523) and break offline. The E-FEEDBACK-3 ReportPageView widgets bind comparison.serverVariable identically, reading completedSession.variables handed to CompletionScreen. FLOW: findMatchingFlowRule (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1541) evaluates rule.condition through variableEngine.evaluateFormula — a scalar server variable participates directly ('score_total > cohortMeanAnxiety' branches offline); object bundles are reachable as 'cohortAnxiety.mean' via mathjs member access (guarded by a scripting-engine test). PIPING: interpolateVariables (variableInterpolation.ts:13) hits the simple-reference fast path for {{cohortMeanAnxiety}} and the AST-evaluator path for {{round(cohortAnxiety.mean, 1)}} — the variables record passed in is getAllVariables() output, which includes server values. QUOTAS: QuotaService.evaluateQuotaCondition parses 'variableName == value' against the same record, so scalar server variables (e.g. cohortCountFemales) gate quota cells offline with last-synced counts. SCRIPTING/interpretive text: same engine, same scope. Persistence: complete() snapshots every variable — server values included — into session.variables (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1638-1645), which OfflineResponsePersistence saves and FilloutUploadSync drains, so analysis datasets record exactly which cohort values the participant was shown.
migration: Two additive, zero-risk migrations plus a contracts regen. (1) SQL: apps/server/migrations/00030_dataset_stats.sql (verified current head: 00029_sessions_insert_user_id_guard.sql) creates ONLY the SECURITY DEFINER function fillout_dataset_stats — no table, no RLS change, no backfill, 00028's fillout_cohort_stats untouched so live 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' modes keep working. Runs automatically via sqlx::migrate! on the qdesigner migration pool at startup. Rollback = DROP FUNCTION. (2) Dexie: version(5) in apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts (current: version(4) at :253) adds filloutServerVariables with all v4 stores restated unchanged and no .upgrade() body — Dexie creates it empty; old clients upgrade lazily on next open; no primary-key changes. (3) Type additions in questionnaire-core are optional fields — existing definitions parse unchanged; definitions without server variables yield an empty /server-variables response and zero fetch cost (the page-load call short-circuits when collectServerVariables(definition) is empty). No data migration: pre-existing sessions, snapshots, and the cohort-stats endpoint are untouched. Coexistence checkpoint: the old 'cohort' source mode remains functional-but-online-only until designers migrate; a later cleanup ADR can deprecate it.

implementation_steps.step: 1. Core types + pure helpers (foundation for everything)
implementation_steps.detail: Add ServerComputationDef/DatasetFilter/ServerStat and Variable.server to the Variable interface; add ReportPageConfig/ReportWidget under QuestionnaireSettings.report. New packages/questionnaire-core/src/serverVariables.ts with collectServerVariables(), declHash() (canonical stable-key JSON hash), materializeServerValue(); export from the package index. Unit-test declHash stability and materialization here — designer, web runtime, and sync all consume these.

implementation_steps.step: 2. Scripting-engine guard tests (prove addressability before building on it)
implementation_steps.detail: Add VariableEngine tests: (a) object-valued variable set via setVariable resolves member access 'cohortAnxiety.mean > 40' in evaluateFormula under createSandboxedMath; (b) scalar server variable participates in evaluateCondition; (c) unset server variable falls back to defaultValue. If (a) fails against the sandboxed mathjs, pivot NOW: auto-register scalar companion variables per stat (cohortAnxiety_mean) in the runtime injection pass instead of relying on member access — this decision gates the flow/piping story.

Generalize 00028's function body: both source branches' vals CTE + JSONB coercion verbatim, add p10/p25/p75 percentile_cont outputs, add p_filter jsonb predicates (versionScope against s.questionnaire_version_*, completedAfter/Before on s.completed_at, where[] → EXISTS over session_variable_index per clause). Same hardening block: SECURITY DEFINER, SET search_path = public, pg_temp, OWNER qdesigner, REVOKE FROM PUBLIC, GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app.

implementation_steps.step: 4. Server endpoint + route + Redis cache + OpenAPI
implementation_steps.detail: ServerVariablesResponse/ServerVariableEntry in models.rs; public_server_variables in analytics.rs (published gate → definition resolve incl. questionnaire_snapshots for ?version → extract content->'variables' entries with server key, cap 50 → fillout_dataset_stats per decl on &state.pool → MIN_COHORT_N floor → respond with computed_at + decl_hash); route beside :180 in api/mod.rs; srvvars:{qid}:{version} Redis TTL ~300s wrapper; openapi.rs registration; regenerate @qdesigner/contracts. cargo check + integration test seed via the DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS get_test_pool pattern.
Add version(5) restating all v4 stores plus filloutServerVariables: '[definitionKey+variableId], definitionKey, questionnaireId, syncedAt'; export the row type. No upgrade body.

Fetch → declHash-verify → bulkPut keyed [definitionKey+variableId]; freshness skip via settings.report?.refreshMs ?? 24h; short-circuit (no network) when collectServerVariables(definition) is empty; pruneDefinitions() deletes rows for GC'd definitionKeys.

implementation_steps.step: 7. Fetch points: page load + pin resume + post-drain
implementation_steps.detail: +page.ts online branch after cacheQuestionnaire (:64): awaited Promise.race with 3s cap, errors swallowed; pin branch (:181-203): cacheServerVariables(effective, pin) when online. FilloutUploadSync.syncNow(): fire-and-forget refresh after a successful drain.

RuntimeConfig (:52) + FilloutRuntimeConfig (:12) gain serverVariables; +page.svelte builds the snapshot Record from one Dexie read (with declHash cross-version fallback) before the FilloutRuntime construction at :584 and threads it through; initializeVariables() (:583) injection pass calls materializeServerValue + setVariable(id, value, 'server-sync'), registers _serverDataAsOf. This step completes the north star: cohortMeanAnxiety resolves offline in flow/piping/quotas with zero consumer diffs.

Add the sourceMode, config fields, validation branch, and the data branch that maps the in-engine stats object into the existing participant-vs-cohort ChartSeriesContract (no fetch); layered fallbacks: n≥5 render → n<5 count-message → fallbackNormTableId (E-FEEDBACK-2 normTables path) → message/hide.

New ReportPageView.svelte composing typed widgets on the 12-col grid (WidgetPosition shape copied from analytics/types/dashboard-builder.ts, not imported); reuses statistical-feedback charts + ScoreInterpreter + ReportGenerator PDF; mounts inside CompletionScreen (outside the runtime item loop — plain Svelte, no FormQuestionHost). Fix the dead wires at +page.svelte:889: pass variables from completedSession.variables + reportConfig=rawDefinition.settings?.report.

VariableManager server-computation editor (mode selector, key picker, stat/type coherence, dataset builder, required defaultValue, cap-50 + identifier validation); StatisticalFeedbackDesigner 'server-variable' source option; new ReportPageEditor panel for settings.report.

implementation_steps.step: 12. Full verification gate + live offline QA
implementation_steps.detail: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check/test/build, scripting-engine test, cargo check/build/test --include-ignored; then the mandatory live-browser drill (per MEMORY 'Live QA is a required gate'): publish a questionnaire with cohortAnxiety (object) + cohortMeanAnxiety (number) + a flow rule + a {{cohortMeanAnxiety}} piping + report box widget; load /q/[code] online once; DevTools → offline; complete the fillout; verify branch taken, piped text, and the box chart with cohort band + 'as of' caption all render with the network tab silent.

test_plan: Server integration (apps/server/tests/server_variables.rs, seeding via the DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS get_test_pool pattern from tests/rls_enforcement.rs): fillout_dataset_stats returns correct n/mean/sd/quartiles for seeded completed sessions; versionScope 'sameMajor'/'exact' excludes other-version sessions; where-clause over session_variable_index filters (eq/in, numeric + string); completedAfter/Before bounds; incomplete sessions excluded; endpoint returns 404 for unpublished/missing, stats:null + sample_count for n<5, respects the 50-declaration cap, resolves ?version= from questionnaire_snapshots, and sets fallback_registry when the snapshot is gone.

scripting-engine (packages/scripting-engine): object-variable member access in formulas under createSandboxedMath ('cohortAnxiety.mean > 40'); scalar server variable in evaluateCondition; defaultValue fallback when never set; importState/exportState round-trips injected 'server-sync' values (resume path).

questionnaire-core: declHash stable across key order / whitespace, changes on any semantic field; collectServerVariables cap + dedupe; materializeServerValue scalar vs object vs below-floor(undefined).

web unit (vitest): Dexie v5 opens over a v4 database with fillout rows intact; cacheServerVariables writes [definitionKey+variableId] rows, skips network within refreshMs, short-circuits on zero declarations; pruneDefinitions deletes orphaned server-variable rows but not protected keys; +page.svelte snapshot builder prefers exact definitionKey and only falls back cross-version on declHash match; QuestionnaireRuntime injection pass sets values with source 'server-sync' and leaves defaultValue when snapshot absent.

Consumer regression tests: flow — a FlowControl rule 'cohortMeanAnxiety > 40' branches correctly with an injected value and falls through on defaultValue; piping — interpolateVariables resolves {{cohortMeanAnxiety}} (fast path) and {{round(cohortAnxiety.mean,1)}} (AST path); quotas — evaluateQuotaCondition against an injected scalar; statistical-feedback engine 'server-variable' mode produces the participant-vs-cohort series with ZERO fetch calls (spy on fetch), and walks the n<5 → norm-table → message fallback chain.
Contract/regression gate: full baseline suites stay green (web 43 files/716, scripting-engine 6/223, server 44 + new file); pnpm build (prerender link check) passes with the new designer panel.

Live QA (required, real browser via agent-browser/chromium): the end-to-end offline drill from step 12 — online first load, force offline, complete fillout, verify flow branch + piped text + report box chart (cohort band, participant marker, n/as-of caption) with the network tab empty; then reconnect, let FilloutUploadSync drain, reload, and verify the refreshed n increments (auto-recalculation loop closed).
risks: mathjs member access on plain objects inside createSandboxedMath may be restricted (the P2-T2 sandbox hardened accessor behavior) — step 2 tests this FIRST; the fallback (auto-registered scalar companions per stat, e.g. cohortAnxiety_mean, mirroring how score.<id> dotted names are walker-only today) is designed in but costs extra registry entries.

Designer-side deanonymization: a malicious/careless designer can publish narrow dataset filters and difference cohorts across versions to bracket an individual. The n≥5 floor and publish-only evaluation bound it, but v1 should also refuse where-clauses on free-text variables in the designer validator and note the residual risk in the ADR.

Below-floor / never-synced semantics leak into FLOW: a branch on cohortMeanAnxiety silently takes the defaultValue path on a fresh offline device. Mitigation: VariableManager requires defaultValue for server variables and the flow-rule editor warns when a condition references one; still a product-behavior footgun to document.

Cohort lag: session_variable_index only reflects DRAINED sessions, so offline completions don't update cohorts until upload; combined with the 300s Redis TTL and 24h client refresh window, participants may see values days old — computedAt captions and staleAfterMs warnings are honesty measures, not fixes.

settings.report and Variable.server ride the definition JSONB through Yjs collaboration — concurrent designer edits to the same variable's dataset filter merge at Yjs granularity; declHash changes then orphan cached rows (correct but wasteful). Low risk, worth a collab smoke test.

Endpoint cost under the 50-declaration cap: a cold cache fans out 50 sequential SECURITY DEFINER scans; with many published questionnaires this needs the Redis cache healthy — the in-memory fallback (rate-limiter pattern) must be verified under Redis outage, else a load spike hits Postgres directly.

questionnaire_snapshots gaps: pinned sessions whose snapshot was never written (pre-00010 data) fall back to the latest registry — declHash mismatch then correctly blocks reuse, but the participant silently gets no cohort values; acceptable, log it.
Dexie version(5) bump is additive but any typo in restated v4 stores corrupts upgrade paths — the v4-open-over-v5 test in the plan is the guard.

Scope creep guard: the old 'cohort'/'participant-vs-cohort' live-fetch modes stay untouched this arc; migrating them onto server variables is a follow-up, otherwise this plan swallows a second refactor.
